name: The paths of Labros and Santa Catalina — circular from Milmarcos (PR-GU 11)
name_original: Los caminos de Labros y Santa Catalina — circular desde Milmarcos (PR-GU 11)
region_intro: Hiking around Milmarcos offers exploration within the "Parameras de Maranchón" Natura 2000 network site, characterized by a mosaic of valleys, hills, and remnants of old agricultural lands. The region features extensive juniper forests and thorn scrub, creating unique ecosystems. This border landscape transitions between high Castilian plateaus and Mediterranean forests, contributing to varied topography and diverse vegetation including sabinas, junipers, and oaks. The local terrain provides opportunities for immersive outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Milmarcos The most popular hiking route is Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular from Jaraba Sanctuary, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and diverse natural scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is The paths of Labros and Santa Catalina — circular from Milmarcos (PR-GU 11), a difficult 15.9 miles (25.6 km) path. This trail offers expansive views and traverses varied terrain, including forested sections. Local hikers also love the Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Jaraba, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through natural landscapes with a waterfall feature, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Milmarcos is defined by juniper forests, varied hills, and a mix of Castilian plateau and Mediterranean forest vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Milmarcos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.97 stars from over 27 reviews. More than 170 hikers have used komoot to explore Milmarcos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Milmarcos, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Milmarcos offers a selection of 14 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 2 easy trails, 10 moderate trails, and 2 difficult routes for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Milmarcos?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, you can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025505715/the-paths-of-labros-and-santa-catalina-circular-from-milmarcos-pr-gu-11" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The paths of Labros and Santa Catalina — circular from Milmarcos (PR-GU 11)&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 15.9-mile loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974346145/rock-paintings-of-roca-benedi-and-calmarza-waterfall-circular-from-jaraba-sanctuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10 miles long and also considered difficult.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Milmarcos?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter extensive juniper forests and thorn scrub, which are unique ecosystems. Hikers can also discover significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4654306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5362060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Mesa River&lt;/a&gt;. The area's border landscape, where Castilian plateaus meet Mediterranean forests, offers varied topography and diverse vegetation including sabinas, junipers, and oaks.

Q: Are there any hiking trails that feature waterfalls near Milmarcos?
A: Absolutely! The area boasts several beautiful waterfalls. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Redondo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar de Mesa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that includes a waterfall feature.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on the trails around Milmarcos?
A: Milmarcos is a haven for diverse wildlife, making it excellent for nature observation. Birdwatchers can spot species like curlews, field pipits, common larks, and Dupont's larks, as well as raptors such as griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, golden eagles, and peregrine falcons. Mammals like foxes, wild boars, and roe deer also inhabit the region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Milmarcos?
A: The hiking routes in Milmarcos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.97 stars from over 27 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique natural scenery, including the extensive juniper forests and varied terrain, and the opportunities for immersive outdoor activities within the Natura 2000 network site.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Milmarcos?
A: Yes, Milmarcos offers 2 easy trails that are suitable for families. These routes provide a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes, allowing everyone to enjoy the unique ecosystems and diverse vegetation without overly strenuous climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Milmarcos?
A: Given the region's extreme continental climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Milmarcos. The temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape, with its juniper forests and varied flora, is particularly vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Milmarcos suitable for dog walking?
A: While many trails in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always advisable to check specific route regulations, especially within protected areas like the Parameras de Maranchón Natura 2000 site. Due to the presence of diverse wildlife, keeping dogs on a leash is often recommended to protect local fauna and ensure their safety.

Q: What is the typical terrain and elevation gain like on Milmarcos hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Milmarcos is characterized by a mosaic of valleys, hills, and a mix of Castilian plateau and Mediterranean forest vegetation. You can expect varied topography. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025505715/the-paths-of-labros-and-santa-catalina-circular-from-milmarcos-pr-gu-11" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The paths of Labros and Santa Catalina&lt;/a&gt; route involves an elevation gain of over 500 meters, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of nearly 300 meters.

Q: Are there any religious sites or sanctuaries accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that leads to this significant religious site, offering both natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: What are some less strenuous hiking options for beginners or those looking for an easier walk?
A: For an easier experience, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34337199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Jaraba – Shepherds' Shelters loop from Jaraba&lt;/a&gt;. This is a moderate 5.2-mile trail that takes about 2 hours 37 minutes, offering pleasant natural landscapes without extreme difficulty. There are also 2 designated easy trails in the region.
tips: Small Catholic sanctuary located in the municipality of Tartanedo, in rural Romanesque style built in the second half of the 12th century.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Santa Catalina of Tartanedo
